warbands.name: Reikland Mercenaries
warbands.description: Hardened soldiers from the Reikland province, well-equipped and disciplined.

warbands.name: Middenheim Mercenaries
warbands.description: Fierce warriors from the city of the White Wolf, known for their ferocity in combat.

warbands.name: Marienburg Mercenaries
warbands.description: Wealthy merchants and their hired soldiers from the richest city in the Old World.

warbands.name: Witch Hunters
warbands.description: Fanatical servants of Sigmar, dedicated to purging evil and corruption.

warbands.name: Sisters of Sigmar
warbands.description: Holy warrior women of Sigmar, fighting to cleanse the cursed city.

warbands.name: The Undead
warbands.description: Risen dead and vampiric lords seeking the cursed wyrdstone.

warbands.name: Cult of the Possessed
warbands.description: Chaos worshippers drawn to Mordheim by the power of the wyrdstone.

warbands.name: Skaven of Clan Eshin
warbands.description: Ratmen from beneath the earth, seeking wyrdstone for their vile experiments.

warbands.name: The Restless Dead
warbands.description: A Liche and its undead legions, ancient skeletal warriors and shambling corpses animated by dark necromancy.

warbands.name: Dark Elves
warbands.description: Cruel raiders from Naggaroth, stealthy and merciless. They venture forth in search of slaves and arcane artefacts.

warbands.name: Shadow Warriors
warbands.description: High Elf Shadow Warriors from Nagarythe, sworn enemies of the Dark Elves. Masters of stealth and archery.

warbands.name: Averlander Mercenaries
warbands.description: Hardy mountain folk from Averland, skilled hunters and trappers who venture into Mordheim seeking fortune.

warbands.name: Beastmen Raiders
warbands.description: Savage beastmen from the deep forests, drawn to Mordheim by the power of wyrdstone and the will of the Dark Gods.

warbands.name: Carnival of Chaos
warbands.description: A twisted travelling carnival devoted to Nurgle, spreading plague and corruption wherever they perform.

warbands.name: Dwarf Treasure Hunters
warbands.description: Stalwart Dwarfs who delve into the ruins of Mordheim seeking lost treasures and ancient artefacts of their people.

warbands.name: Kislevites
warbands.description: Fierce warriors from the frozen north of Kislev, hardened by bitter cold and constant war against the forces of Chaos.

warbands.name: Orc Mob
warbands.description: Brutal greenskins drawn to Mordheim by the promise of loot, fighting and wyrdstone to sell to the highest bidder.

warbands.name: Ostlander Mercenaries
warbands.description: Tough, no-nonsense folk from the eastern Empire province of Ostland, renowned for their stubbornness and resilience.

warbands.name: Pit Fighters
warbands.description: Escaped gladiatorial fighters from the brutal fighting pits, specialists in close-quarters combat.

warbands.name: Skaven of Clan Pestilens
warbands.description: Disease-ridden Skaven devoted to plague and pestilence, spreading contagion through the streets of Mordheim.

warbands.name: Tileans
warbands.description: Mercenary warbands from the wealthy city-states of Tilea. Variants: Miraglean (+1 BS crossbow, hate Skaven), Remasen (re-roll rout vs Dark Elves, +1 Ld heroes), Trantio (+100gc starting gold).

warbands.name: Tomb Guardians
warbands.description: Ancient Nehekharan undead warriors guarding the tombs and pyramids of the Land of the Dead.

warbands.name: Amazons (Lustria)
warbands.description: Fierce warrior women from the jungles of Lustria, devoted to the Old Ones and skilled in ritual combat.

warbands.name: Amazons (Mordheim)
warbands.description: Amazon warriors who have ventured far from Lustria to seek wyrdstone in the ruins of Mordheim.

warbands.name: Arabian Tomb Raiders
warbands.description: Desert nomads and treasure seekers from the lands of Araby, drawn to Mordheim by tales of wyrdstone riches.

warbands.name: Black Orcs
warbands.description: Elite greenskin warriors, larger and more disciplined than common orcs, seeking the Nemesis Crown.

warbands.name: Bretonnians
warbands.description: Noble knights and their retinues from Bretonnia, bound by chivalric oaths and the quest for the Lady's favour.

warbands.name: Dwarf Rangers
warbands.description: Doughty dwarf rangers patrolling the wild lands, protecting their holds and hunting greenskins.

warbands.name: Forest Goblins
warbands.description: Cunning forest goblins who ride giant spiders and use primitive but deadly tactics in the deep woods.

warbands.name: Gunnery School of Nuln
warbands.description: Students and instructors from the Imperial Gunnery School, bringing black powder superiority to the battlefield.

warbands.name: Hochland Bandits
warbands.description: Cunning outlaws and highwaymen from the forests of Hochland, preying on travellers and rival warbands.

warbands.name: Horned Hunters
warbands.description: Zealous hunters devoted to Taal, the god of nature, tracking bandits and chaos through the wild forests.

warbands.name: Lizardmen
warbands.description: Ancient reptilian warriors from the temple-cities of Lustria, executing the Great Plan of the Old Ones.

warbands.name: Norse Explorers
warbands.description: Norse seafarers and raiders exploring Lustria for riches and glory.

warbands.name: Mootlanders
warbands.description: Halfling warbands from the Moot seeking gold and abandoned kitchens.

warbands.name: Imperial Outriders
warbands.description: Swift mounted scouts dispatched by Imperial provinces to explore the Great Forest.

warbands.name: Outlaws of Stirwood Forest
warbands.description: Forest outlaws and bandits lurking in Stirwood Forest near Mordheim.

warbands.name: Pirates
warbands.description: Seafaring raiders and plunderers navigating the rivers near Mordheim.
